title: Bartleby the Scrivener A Story of Wall Street
author: Herman Melville
prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is verbatim from the "Bartleby the Scrivener A Story of Wall Street" book by Herman Melville?
Options:
A. I admit it was somewhat feeble of me, but his attitude irritated me this time. Not only did he seem to have a certain tranquil disrespect, but his obstinacy appeared unthankful, given the undoubtable good treatment and leniency he had received from me. I sat thinking again about what action I should take. Although I felt humiliated by his conduct, and had decided to fire him when I entered my office, I strangely felt something superstitious rapping at my heart, and stopping me from carrying out my intent, and calling me a scoundrel if I dared utter one bitter word against this most pitiable of men.
B. I concede it was fairly weak of me, but his manner annoyed me on this event. There appeared to be not just a specific composed disdain, but also his stubbornness seemed unappreciative, considering the clear good usage and tolerance he had gotten from me. I sat pondering again what I ought to do. Despite feeling embarrassed by his actions, and having resolved to dismiss him when I went into my office, oddly I felt something mystical knocking at my heart, and preventing me from implementing my purpose, and labeling me a villain if I tried to say one harsh word against this most wretched of people.
C. I acknowledge it was somewhat feeble of me, but his attitude irritated me this time. There seemed to lurk not just a certain calm disrespect, but also his obstinacy appeared ungrateful, given the clear good treatment and leniency he had received from me. I sat thinking again about what I should do. Although I felt ashamed by his behavior, and had decided to fire him when I entered my office, strangely I felt something mystical rapping at my heart, and hindering me from carrying out my intent, and calling me a scoundrel if I dared utter one bitter word against this most pitiful of individuals.
D. It was rather weak in me I confess, but his manner on this occasion nettled me. Not only did there seem to lurk in it a certain calm disdain, but his perverseness seemed ungrateful, considering the undeniable good usage and indulgence he had received from me. Again I sat ruminating what I should do. Mortified as I was at his behavior, and resolved as I had been to dismiss him when I entered my offices, nevertheless I strangely felt something superstitious knocking at my heart, and forbidding me to carry out my purpose, and denouncing me for a villain if I dared to breathe one bitter word against this forlornest of mankind.
Answer:

options.A: I admit it was somewhat feeble of me, but his attitude irritated me this time. Not only did he seem to have a certain tranquil disrespect, but his obstinacy appeared unthankful, given the undoubtable good treatment and leniency he had received from me. I sat thinking again about what action I should take. Although I felt humiliated by his conduct, and had decided to fire him when I entered my office, I strangely felt something superstitious rapping at my heart, and stopping me from carrying out my intent, and calling me a scoundrel if I dared utter one bitter word against this most pitiable of men.
options.B: I concede it was fairly weak of me, but his manner annoyed me on this event. There appeared to be not just a specific composed disdain, but also his stubbornness seemed unappreciative, considering the clear good usage and tolerance he had gotten from me. I sat pondering again what I ought to do. Despite feeling embarrassed by his actions, and having resolved to dismiss him when I went into my office, oddly I felt something mystical knocking at my heart, and preventing me from implementing my purpose, and labeling me a villain if I tried to say one harsh word against this most wretched of people.
options.C: I acknowledge it was somewhat feeble of me, but his attitude irritated me this time. There seemed to lurk not just a certain calm disrespect, but also his obstinacy appeared ungrateful, given the clear good treatment and leniency he had received from me. I sat thinking again about what I should do. Although I felt ashamed by his behavior, and had decided to fire him when I entered my office, strangely I felt something mystical rapping at my heart, and hindering me from carrying out my intent, and calling me a scoundrel if I dared utter one bitter word against this most pitiful of individuals.
options.D: It was rather weak in me I confess, but his manner on this occasion nettled me. Not only did there seem to lurk in it a certain calm disdain, but his perverseness seemed ungrateful, considering the undeniable good usage and indulgence he had received from me. Again I sat ruminating what I should do. Mortified as I was at his behavior, and resolved as I had been to dismiss him when I entered my offices, nevertheless I strangely felt something superstitious knocking at my heart, and forbidding me to carry out my purpose, and denouncing me for a villain if I dared to breathe one bitter word against this forlornest of mankind.
